1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"fmt"
8		"net"
9		"net/http"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"runtime"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15		"testing"
16		"time"
17	
18		"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
19	
20		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/metrics"
21	)
22	
23	// P2 (aggregation + data-thrift) e2e tuning. These are derived from the emulated
24	// per-path rate cap and the inner MTU, and are the levers the on-hardware operator
25	// retunes if the fixture's achievable frame rate differs from the emulation.
26	//
27	// WHY A RATE CAP IS MANDATORY (the T23 well-definedness note). The fixture's bottleneck
28	// is the single userspace WireGuard crypto core — one syscall per datagram on one vCPU,
29	// SHARED by both paths — NOT the emulated links. So bonding two UNCAPPED paths does not
30	// raise throughput (the CPU ceiling is unchanged), making "sum of the two paths'
31	// individual throughputs" ill-defined and the aggregation ratio vacuous. Capping each
32	// path well BELOW that in-fixture CPU ceiling makes the LINK the bottleneck: a solo capped
33	// path saturates at ~its cap, and a bonded run can approach the SUM of the two caps while
34	// the aggregate stays under the ceiling. Only then is the >= P2BondedMinFraction ratio a
35	// real aggregation measurement rather than a CPU-ceiling artefact.
36	//
37	// THE SINGLE-HOST FIXTURE IS CPU/PPS-BOUND — the bonded ratio is SKIPPED here, enforced
38	// elsewhere. Measured on hardware (llm-ubuntu-0, 4-vCPU amd64): a solo capped path reaches
39	// only ~4–13 Mbit/s (starlink) / ~2–3 Mbit/s (cellular) at a 40-Mbit cap, and this does NOT
40	// rise with parallel TCP streams — it is packet-processing-bound (two userspace-WireGuard
41	// daemons + the load generator + netem all sharing the host's cores), not single-flow
42	// congestion-bound. Because BOTH daemons share one host's CPU, bonding two paths cannot
43	// exceed the single-path ceiling by their sum, so "bonded >= 0.85 * (soloA+soloB)" measures
44	// the shared-CPU ceiling, not aggregation. (Do NOT confuse the low in-fixture figure with the
45	// ~150–170 Mbit/s number quoted elsewhere — that is the REAL-INTERNET cross-host measurement,
46	// one daemon per host with independent cores, .cq/goals.md G1, a different regime.) THEREFORE:
47	// runSoloSaturated measures each path and, when a solo is not link-bound (< p2SoloSaturation
48	// Fraction of the cap), the bonded-aggregation subtest SKIPS with the measured evidence rather
49	// than assert a quantity the fixture cannot produce. The bonded-throughput proof belongs to a
50	// real two-host setup with INDEPENDENT per-path capacity (the deferred final hardware step);
51	// on any link-bound venue the ratio IS enforced. What this test DOES enforce in-fixture: the
52	// data-thrift (5G-idle) assertion. Aggregation PROPORTIONALITY is covered by the weighted
53	// scheduler's unit tests (internal/sched/weighted_test.go).
54	const (
55		// p2RateMbit is the per-path netem egress cap. For the bonded-aggregation ratio to be a
56		// valid measurement (rather than SKIPPED), each solo path must saturate this cap, i.e. it
57		// must sit below the executing host's in-fixture per-path CPU/PPS ceiling. On the available
58		// single-host fixtures that ceiling is only a few Mbit/s (see the note above), so the ratio
59		// subtest skips there; the cap remains meaningful for a link-bound venue. Lower it until
60		// each solo saturates it if you want the ratio enforced on a beefier host.
61		p2RateMbit = 40
62	
63		// p2MetricsListen is the loopback /metrics endpoint both daemons bind (each in its own
64		// network namespace, so the identical address does not collide). p2MetricsURL is the
65		// scrape URL metrics.Fetch drives.
66		p2MetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9095"
67		p2MetricsURL    = "http://" + p2MetricsListen + "/metrics"
68	
69		// p2PerPathCapacityFPS sets the weighted scheduler's aggregation gate. Aggregation
70		// engages once the offered frame rate exceeds EngageFraction(0.9)*capacity and
71		// collapses to primary-only below DisengageFraction(0.5)*capacity. It is chosen so a
72		// SINGLE saturated capped path (~p2RateMbit/8/innerMTU ≈ 3400 fps at 40 Mbit/s and a
73		// ~1400-byte inner MTU) sits ABOVE the engage threshold — so a bonded saturating flow
74		// (which first fills the primary, driving offered load past engage) opens the backup —
75		// while a sub-capacity thrift flow stays below the disengage threshold and collapses.
76		// 3000 fps => engage at 2700 (< ~3400 one-path fps: engages under bond) and disengage
77		// at 1500 (a <15 Mbit/s thrift flow, ~1300 fps, stays collapsed). Retune with the cap.
78		p2PerPathCapacityFPS = 3000.0
79	
80		// p2LoadSecs is the saturating flow duration; p2WindowSettle/p2WindowSecs carve the
81		// steady-state measurement window out of the middle so ramp-up and tail are excluded.
82		p2LoadSecs     = 16
83		p2WindowSettle = 5 * time.Second
84		p2WindowSecs   = 7
85	
86		// p2ThriftBitrate is the sub-one-path-capacity offered load for the data-thrift phase:
87		// low enough that the weighted gate stays collapsed on the primary, so the metered
88		// (5G/cellular) path carries < P2MeteredMaxByteFraction of DATA bytes.
89		p2ThriftBitrate = "12M"
90		p2ThriftSecs    = 12
91	
92		// p2SoloSaturationFraction is the fraction of the per-path cap a SOLO run must reach for
93		// the fixture to count as LINK-bound (not CPU-bound) on the executing host — the
94		// precondition that makes the bonded >= P2BondedMinFraction*(soloA+soloB) assertion
95		// well-defined. It doubles as the guard against a vacuous pass: with each solo floored at
96		// p2SoloSaturationFraction*p2RateMbit, the bonded target want = P2BondedMinFraction *
97		// (soloA+soloB) >= 0.85 * 2 * 0.9 * p2RateMbit = 1.53 * p2RateMbit, which STRICTLY EXCEEDS
98		// the single-path cap p2RateMbit (1.53 > 1). So NO single path — capped at p2RateMbit —
99		// can ever satisfy the bonded assertion; only genuine two-path aggregation can. The ~0.98
100		// header/ACK gap between the netem cap (outer IP datagram) and the measured DATA-frame
101		// wire throughput leaves ample margin under 0.9.
102		p2SoloSaturationFraction = 0.9
103	
104		// p2StripingCapacityFPS is the FIXTURE-SCALED aggregation gate for the FUNCTIONAL
105		// bonded-striping subtest (TestP2Aggregation/bonded-striping) — separate from the ratio
106		// path's p2PerPathCapacityFPS=3000, which is unchanged. The striping subtest proves the
107		// weighted scheduler drives two REAL per-path UDP sockets concurrently end-to-end; it must
108		// therefore reliably ENGAGE aggregation even on the CPU/PPS-bound single-host fixture,
109		// where the delivered frame rate is low. Aggregation engages once the smoothed offered
110		// frame rate exceeds EngageFraction(0.9)*capacity = 0.9*40 = 36 fps. The WORST observed
111		// in-fixture delivered rate is ~54 fps (the 0.6 Mbit/s low end at ~1400-B frames; the
112		// range runs ~54–1160 fps at 0.6–13 Mbit/s per the hardware run). 36 < 54, so a saturating
113		// flow's offered load clears the engage threshold with margin on every measured venue and
114		// the backup socket is reliably opened — WITHOUT any throughput-ratio assertion, so the
115		// subtest is robust to the fixture's CPU-boundedness. Pacing is OFF (config default), so a
116		// low capacity only lowers the engage/disengage thresholds; it never sheds frames.
117		p2StripingCapacityFPS = 40.0
118	
119		// p2StripingMinRxBytes is the per-path far-end DATA-carriage floor for the striping
120		// subtest. The concentrator's rx counter (readLoop) counts ALL received outer bytes —
121		// including the per-path liveness PROBES/echoes the peer sends on EVERY path — so a bare
122		// "conc rx > 0" is VACUOUS (satisfied by probes with zero DATA striping). Probe+echo
123		// traffic over the ~p2WindowSecs (7 s) window is < ~20 KB/path (a few frames/s of small
124		// probes, both directions). This 50 KB floor sits far above that noise yet far below the
125		// DATA a saturating striped flow delivers on the backup even at the low in-fixture rates
126		// (hundreds of KB to MBs over the window), so clearing it PROVES real DATA reassembly on
127		// that far-end socket, not probe chatter.
128		p2StripingMinRxBytes = 50_000
129	)
130	
131	// TestP2Aggregation is the P2 acceptance (T23): under saturating load the bonded
132	// throughput reaches at least P2BondedMinFraction of the sum of the two paths' INDIVIDUAL
133	// (solo) throughputs, and while the primary (Starlink) is healthy a sub-capacity flow
134	// keeps the metered backup (5G/cellular) below P2MeteredMaxByteFraction of DATA bytes.
135	// BOTH quantities are read from the daemon's /metrics endpoint (via metrics.Fetch), not
136	// from iperf3 or a packet capture — the endpoint is the operator-facing surface this
137	// phase certifies.
138	//
139	// Interpretation of "bonded >= 0.85 * sum of the two paths' individual throughputs"
140	// (documented because the phrasing admits more than one reading): each path's INDIVIDUAL
141	// throughput is measured in its OWN solo run (only that path configured), and the bonded
142	// AGGREGATE wire throughput (both paths, from /metrics) is compared against 0.85 * their
143	// sum. All three quantities are per-path OUTER-wire throughput read from /metrics
144	// (wanbond_path_tx/rx_bytes_total deltas), so the comparison is dimensionally consistent
145	// (wire-vs-wire) and robust: it directly tests that bonding preserves each path's solo
146	// capacity (aggregation efficiency), rather than folding the goodput/wire framing overhead
147	// into the threshold. The subtests run sequentially (no t.Parallel) so each solo topology
148	// is torn down before the next Setup — the fixed veth names forbid two live topologies.
149	func TestP2Aggregation(t *testing.T) {
150		bin := buildWanbond(t)
151	
152		var soloStarlink, soloCellular float64
153		t.Run("solo-starlink", func(t *testing.T) {
154			soloStarlink = runSoloSaturated(t, bin, "starlink")
155			t.Logf("solo starlink wire throughput = %.1f Mbit/s", soloStarlink)
156		})
157		t.Run("solo-cellular", func(t *testing.T) {
158			soloCellular = runSoloSaturated(t, bin, "cellular")
159			t.Logf("solo cellular wire throughput = %.1f Mbit/s", soloCellular)
160		})
161	
162		t.Run("bonded-aggregation", func(t *testing.T) {
163			if soloStarlink <= 0 || soloCellular <= 0 {
164				t.Fatalf("solo baselines not measured (starlink=%.1f cellular=%.1f); cannot judge aggregation",
165					soloStarlink, soloCellular)
166			}
167			// The bonded-throughput ratio is only a valid aggregation measurement when each
168			// solo path is LINK-bound (saturates its cap). On the CPU/PPS-bound single-host
169			// netns fixture the solos fall far below the cap (shared-CPU userspace crypto), so
170			// bonding on one host cannot reach the sum and the ratio measures the CPU ceiling,
171			// not aggregation — SKIP with the measured evidence rather than assert a quantity
172			// the fixture cannot produce. The proof of bonded throughput belongs to a real
173			// two-host setup with INDEPENDENT per-path capacity (the deferred final hardware
174			// step). Aggregation FUNCTIONALITY is covered elsewhere: the weighted scheduler's
175			// proportional split by unit test (internal/sched weighted_test), and the far-end
176			// both-paths-carry-traffic cross-check below when it does run.
177			if !soloIsLinkBound(soloStarlink) || !soloIsLinkBound(soloCellular) {
178				t.Skipf("fixture is CPU/PPS-bound, not link-bound (solo starlink=%.1f cellular=%.1f Mbit/s, both < %.1f = %.2f*%d-Mbit cap): "+
179					"the bonded>=%.2f*sum ratio measures the shared-CPU ceiling here, not aggregation. It is enforced only on a link-bound venue "+
180					"(a host with enough CPU headroom that each solo saturates its cap, or a real two-host setup with independent per-path capacity). "+
181					"Data-thrift (5G-idle) IS enforced in-fixture below; proportional aggregation is covered by the weighted-scheduler unit tests.",
182					soloStarlink, soloCellular, p2SoloSaturationFraction*p2RateMbit, p2SoloSaturationFraction, p2RateMbit, P2BondedMinFraction)
183			}
184			bondedAgg := runBondedSaturated(t, bin)
185			want := P2BondedMinFraction * (soloStarlink + soloCellular)
186			t.Logf("bonded aggregate wire throughput = %.1f Mbit/s; want >= %.1f (%.2f * (%.1f + %.1f))",
187				bondedAgg, want, P2BondedMinFraction, soloStarlink, soloCellular)
188			if bondedAgg < want {
189				t.Fatalf("bonded aggregate throughput %.1f Mbit/s < %.1f Mbit/s (%.0f%% of the solo sum) — aggregation did not reach P2BondedMinFraction",
190					bondedAgg, want, P2BondedMinFraction*100)
191			}
192		})
193	
194		// The FUNCTIONAL aggregation proof, robust to the fixture's CPU/PPS-boundedness (makes
195		// NO throughput-ratio assertion). It is the only test anywhere that exercises the socket
196		// datapath seam "weighted Pick -> two per-path UDP sockets under concurrent load -> far
197		// end reassembles on both": the sched unit tests only prove Pick-INDEX distribution (never
198		// leaving the scheduler), and P1 multipath is active-backup (one socket at a time). With a
199		// fixture-scaled gate that reliably engages (p2StripingCapacityFPS), it asserts the edge
200		// striped DATA onto BOTH sockets AND the concentrator reassembled DATA from both. Unlike
201		// the ratio subtest it PASSES in-fixture — it is what the hardware run must go green on.
202		t.Run("bonded-striping", func(t *testing.T) {
203			runBondedStriping(t, bin)
204		})
205	
206		t.Run("data-thrift", func(t *testing.T) {
207			runDataThrift(t, bin)
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// runSoloSaturated brings the tunnel up over a SINGLE rate-capped path with the weighted
212	// scheduler + /metrics, saturates it, and returns that path's steady-state OUTER-wire
213	// throughput (Mbit/s) read from the edge /metrics byte counters.
214	//
215	// It measures the solo run's throughput. The caller checks whether it is LINK-bound
216	// (>= p2SoloSaturationFraction of the cap) via soloIsLinkBound: that precondition is what
217	// makes the downstream bonded >= P2BondedMinFraction*(soloA+soloB) comparison well-defined.
218	// The single-host netns fixture is CPU/PPS-bound — both userspace-WireGuard daemons plus the
219	// load generator and netem share the host's cores, so measured per-path throughput sits FAR
220	// below the tbf cap (2–13 Mbit/s per path at a 40-Mbit cap on the 4-vCPU amd64 host, and it
221	// does NOT rise with parallel streams — it is packet-processing-bound, not single-flow
222	// congestion-bound). Bonding two paths on ONE shared-CPU host therefore cannot exceed the
223	// single-path ceiling by the sum, so the bonded-throughput RATIO is not measurable in this
224	// fixture; the caller SKIPS that subtest with the measured evidence when not link-bound. The
225	// true bonded-throughput proof requires INDEPENDENT per-path capacity (a real two-host setup
226	// with separate links — the deferred final real-hardware step). What IS enforced in-fixture:
227	// the data-thrift (5G-idle) assertion, and — on any venue where the fixture is link-bound —
228	// the bonded ratio itself.
229	func runSoloSaturated(t *testing.T, bin, name string) float64 {
230		t.Helper()
231		spec := p2Path(name)
232		top := SetupWithPaths(t, []pathSpec{spec})
233		edge, conc := setupP2Tunnel(t, top, bin, []pathSpec{spec}, p2PerPathCapacityFPS)
234		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
235			t.Fatalf("solo %s: tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", name, edge.log(), conc.log())
236		}
237		return top.measureSaturatedWireThroughput(t, []string{name})[name]
238	}
239	
240	// soloIsLinkBound reports whether a solo throughput reached the link cap (within
241	// p2SoloSaturationFraction), i.e. the fixture is link-bound rather than CPU/PPS-bound and the
242	// bonded-aggregation ratio is a valid measurement on this host.
243	func soloIsLinkBound(tput float64) bool { return tput >= p2SoloSaturationFraction*p2RateMbit }
244	
245	// runBondedSaturated brings the tunnel up over BOTH rate-capped paths with the weighted
246	// scheduler, saturates it so aggregation engages, and returns the AGGREGATE (both paths)
247	// OUTER-wire throughput (Mbit/s) from the edge /metrics. It also scrapes the CONCENTRATOR
248	// /metrics (via metrics.Fetch inside the concentrator netns) to satisfy the both-daemons
249	// requirement and to confirm the far end observed both paths carrying the bonded flow.
250	func runBondedSaturated(t *testing.T, bin string) float64 {
251		t.Helper()
252		paths := p2Paths()
253		top := SetupWithPaths(t, paths)
254		edge, conc := setupP2Tunnel(t, top, bin, paths, p2PerPathCapacityFPS)
255		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
256			t.Fatalf("bonded: tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
257		}
258	
259		tputs := top.measureSaturatedWireThroughput(t, []string{"starlink", "cellular"})
260	
261		// Both daemons via metrics.Fetch: the concentrator endpoint is loopback-bound INSIDE
262		// its own netns, so it is scraped from within that netns. Assert its per-path series
263		// are present and both paths received bonded traffic (rx > 0) — the far-end cross-check
264		// that aggregation, not single-path fallback, delivered the flow.
265		concExp := fetchMetricsInNetns(t, top.pid, p2MetricsURL)
266		for _, p := range []string{"starlink", "cellular"} {
267			rx, ok := concExp.PathValue(metrics.MetricRxBytes, p)
268			if !ok {
269				t.Fatalf("concentrator /metrics missing %s{path=%q}", metrics.MetricRxBytes, p)
270			}
271			if rx <= 0 {
272				t.Errorf("concentrator saw 0 rx bytes on path %q — aggregation did not use it", p)
273			}
274		}
275	
276		return tputs["starlink"] + tputs["cellular"]
277	}
278	
279	// runBondedStriping is the FUNCTIONAL two-path-carriage proof (no throughput ratio, robust
280	// to a CPU/PPS-bound fixture). It brings BOTH paths up with a fixture-scaled aggregation
281	// gate (p2StripingCapacityFPS) that reliably engages under the low in-fixture frame rate,
282	// drives a saturating flow, and asserts — over a steady-state window — that:
283	//
284	//   - the edge sent DATA on BOTH per-path sockets (tx counters count DATA frames only, NOT
285	//     the liveness probes/echoes written outside the Send counter, so a positive BACKUP tx
286	//     PROVES the weighted scheduler striped DATA onto the second socket — it is not merely
287	//     probe traffic); and
288	//   - the concentrator RECEIVED and reassembled DATA from BOTH sockets — measured as the
289	//     per-path rx DELTA exceeding p2StripingMinRxBytes, a floor set above probe/echo noise
290	//     (conc rx counts probes too, so a bare rx>0 would be vacuous).
291	//
292	// Together these exercise the end-to-end socket datapath seam the unit tests and the P1
293	// active-backup e2e never reach: weighted Pick -> two concurrent UDP sockets -> far-end
294	// resequenced reassembly on both.
295	func runBondedStriping(t *testing.T, bin string) {
296		t.Helper()
297		paths := p2Paths()
298		top := SetupWithPaths(t, paths)
299		edge, conc := setupP2Tunnel(t, top, bin, paths, p2StripingCapacityFPS)
300		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
301			t.Fatalf("striping: tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
302		}
303	
304		top.startSaturatingLoad(t)
305		time.Sleep(p2WindowSettle) // let the flow ramp and the aggregation gate engage
306	
307		ctxB, cancelB := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
308		defer cancelB()
309		edgeBefore := fetchMetrics(t, ctxB, p2MetricsURL)
310		concBefore := fetchMetricsInNetns(t, top.pid, p2MetricsURL)
311	
312		time.Sleep(p2WindowSecs * time.Second)
313	
314		ctxA, cancelA := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
315		defer cancelA()
316		edgeAfter := fetchMetrics(t, ctxA, p2MetricsURL)
317		concAfter := fetchMetricsInNetns(t, top.pid, p2MetricsURL)
318	
319		// Edge striped DATA onto BOTH sockets under concurrent load (tx = DATA frames only).
320		starTx := deltaPathValue(t, edgeBefore, edgeAfter, metrics.MetricTxBytes, "starlink")
321		cellTx := deltaPathValue(t, edgeBefore, edgeAfter, metrics.MetricTxBytes, "cellular")
322		t.Logf("bonded-striping: edge DATA tx over window — starlink=%.0f B, cellular(backup)=%.0f B", starTx, cellTx)
323		if starTx <= 0 {
324			t.Fatalf("edge sent 0 DATA bytes on the primary under saturating load — no flow\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
325		}
326		if cellTx <= 0 {
327			t.Fatalf("edge sent 0 DATA bytes on the BACKUP (cellular) socket under saturating load — the weighted scheduler did not stripe DATA onto the second socket (aggregation never engaged at gate %.0f fps)\n--- edge ---\n%s",
328				p2StripingCapacityFPS, edge.log())
329		}
330	
331		// Far end reassembled DATA from BOTH sockets (rx delta above the probe-noise floor).
332		starRx := deltaPathValue(t, concBefore, concAfter, metrics.MetricRxBytes, "starlink")
333		cellRx := deltaPathValue(t, concBefore, concAfter, metrics.MetricRxBytes, "cellular")
334		t.Logf("bonded-striping: conc rx over window — starlink=%.0f B, cellular(backup)=%.0f B (floor %d B)", starRx, cellRx, p2StripingMinRxBytes)
335		if starRx < p2StripingMinRxBytes {
336			t.Fatalf("concentrator received only %.0f B DATA on the primary over the window (< %d floor) — far end did not reassemble the primary's traffic\n--- conc ---\n%s",
337				starRx, p2StripingMinRxBytes, conc.log())
338		}
339		if cellRx < p2StripingMinRxBytes {
340			t.Fatalf("concentrator received only %.0f B on the BACKUP socket over the window (< %d floor = probe noise) — far end reassembled no DATA from the second path; concurrent two-path carriage unproven\n--- conc ---\n%s",
341				cellRx, p2StripingMinRxBytes, conc.log())
342		}
343	}
344	
345	// runDataThrift drives a SUB-capacity flow while the primary is healthy and asserts the
346	// metered backup carries < P2MeteredMaxByteFraction of the DATA (tx) bytes, all read from
347	// the edge /metrics. It measures the DELTA over the flow window (not cumulative counters)
348	// so any startup transient before the weighted gate settled is excluded.
349	func runDataThrift(t *testing.T, bin string) {
350		t.Helper()
351		paths := p2Paths()
352		top := SetupWithPaths(t, paths)
353		edge, conc := setupP2Tunnel(t, top, bin, paths, p2PerPathCapacityFPS)
354		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
355			t.Fatalf("thrift: tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
356		}
357	
358		// Let the offered-load meter settle to the idle (collapsed) state before sampling.
359		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
360	
361		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
362		defer cancel()
363		before := fetchMetrics(t, ctx, p2MetricsURL)
364	
365		// Primary must be healthy for the data-thrift guarantee to apply.
366		if up, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, "starlink"); !ok || up != 1 {
367			t.Fatalf("primary (starlink) not healthy at thrift start (up=%v ok=%v) — thrift precondition unmet\n%s",
368				up, ok, edge.log())
369		}
370	
371		// A bandwidth-limited flow that fits comfortably inside one path's capacity, so the
372		// weighted gate stays collapsed on the primary.
373		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-thrift-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
374		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
375		top.run("iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(p2ThriftSecs), "-b", p2ThriftBitrate)
376	
377		ctx2, cancel2 := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
378		defer cancel2()
379		after := fetchMetrics(t, ctx2, p2MetricsURL)
380	
381		starTx := deltaPathValue(t, before, after, metrics.MetricTxBytes, "starlink")
382		cellTx := deltaPathValue(t, before, after, metrics.MetricTxBytes, "cellular")
383		total := starTx + cellTx
384		if total <= 0 {
385			t.Fatalf("no DATA tx observed over the thrift window (starlink=%.0f cellular=%.0f) — flow did not run", starTx, cellTx)
386		}
387		frac := cellTx / total
388		t.Logf("data-thrift: cellular tx = %.0f B, starlink tx = %.0f B, cellular fraction = %.4f (want < %.4f)",
389			cellTx, starTx, frac, P2MeteredMaxByteFraction)
390		if frac >= P2MeteredMaxByteFraction {
391			t.Fatalf("metered (cellular) carried %.2f%% of DATA bytes while primary healthy, want < %.2f%% — data-thrift violated",
392				frac*100, P2MeteredMaxByteFraction*100)
393		}
394	}
395	
396	// measureSaturatedWireThroughput starts a saturating TCP upload, waits for steady state,
397	// scrapes the edge /metrics at the window's start and end, and returns each named path's
398	// OUTER-wire throughput (Mbit/s) = (delta tx+rx bytes)*8 / window. The upload direction
399	// exercises edge egress aggregation; the reverse ACKs are small and counted uniformly in
400	// every run, so they do not skew the wire-vs-wire comparison.
401	func (top *Topology) measureSaturatedWireThroughput(t *testing.T, names []string) map[string]float64 {
402		t.Helper()
403		top.startSaturatingLoad(t)
404	
405		time.Sleep(p2WindowSettle)
406		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
407		defer cancel()
408		before := fetchMetrics(t, ctx, p2MetricsURL)
409		start := time.Now()
410	
411		time.Sleep(p2WindowSecs * time.Second)
412		ctx2, cancel2 := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
413		defer cancel2()
414		after := fetchMetrics(t, ctx2, p2MetricsURL)
415		elapsed := time.Since(start).Seconds()
416	
417		out := make(map[string]float64, len(names))
418		for _, name := range names {
419			txd := deltaPathValue(t, before, after, metrics.MetricTxBytes, name)
420			rxd := deltaPathValue(t, before, after, metrics.MetricRxBytes, name)
421			out[name] = (txd + rxd) * 8 / elapsed / 1e6
422		}
423		return out
424	}
425	
426	// startSaturatingLoad launches a one-shot concentrator-side iperf3 server and a saturating
427	// TCP upload from the edge (p2LoadSecs), both in the background with their own terminating
428	// cleanup. Callers sample /metrics across a steady-state window inside the flow's lifetime.
429	func (top *Topology) startSaturatingLoad(t *testing.T) {
430		t.Helper()
431		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
432		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
433		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(p2LoadSecs))
434	}
435	
436	// deltaPathValue returns after-before for a per-path counter series, failing if either
437	// scrape lacked the series (a missing series is a wiring defect, not a zero).
438	func deltaPathValue(t *testing.T, before, after metrics.Exposition, name, path string) float64 {
439		t.Helper()
440		b, ok := before.PathValue(name, path)
441		if !ok {
442			t.Fatalf("first scrape missing %s{path=%q}", name, path)
443		}
444		a, ok := after.PathValue(name, path)
445		if !ok {
446			t.Fatalf("second scrape missing %s{path=%q}", name, path)
447		}
448		return a - b
449	}
450	
451	// fetchMetrics scrapes an in-namespace /metrics endpoint via metrics.Fetch.
452	func fetchMetrics(t *testing.T, ctx context.Context, url string) metrics.Exposition {
453		t.Helper()
454		exp, err := metrics.Fetch(ctx, http.DefaultClient, url)
455		if err != nil {
456			t.Fatalf("scrape %s: %v", url, err)
457		}
458		return exp
459	}
460	
461	// fetchMetricsInNetns scrapes a loopback /metrics endpoint that lives inside the
462	// concentrator's network namespace, via metrics.Fetch. The socket must be OPENED inside the
463	// peer netns. The subtlety that a prior version got wrong (T25 root-cause): net/http does
464	// its dial on a BACKGROUND goroutine (Transport.dialConnFor), so merely moving the CALLING
465	// thread into the netns (LockOSThread+Setns, then Fetch) does NOT confine the socket — the
466	// dial goroutine runs on a different, ROOT-netns thread and connects to whatever binds the
467	// same loopback address in the ROOT netns. Because the edge daemon binds the IDENTICAL
468	// 127.0.0.1:<port> /metrics in the root netns, the scrape silently read the EDGE endpoint
469	// instead of the concentrator (its per-path series look plausible, so it passed unnoticed
470	// until P3 asserted a concentrator-ONLY quantity — the FEC recovered/unrecoverable counters,
471	// which are ~0 on the edge). The namespace switch therefore belongs in the custom
472	// DialContext — the exact place the socket() syscall runs — on a thread pinned
473	// (runtime.LockOSThread) and moved into the peer netns; that goroutine then EXITS WITHOUT
474	// unlocking, so the Go runtime discards the now-namespace-polluted OS thread rather than
475	// returning it (dirty) to the pool. Only the socket creation is namespace-sensitive; once
476	// connected, the socket's namespace is fixed, so the HTTP read/write may run anywhere. This
477	// needs no /run/netns mount.
478	func fetchMetricsInNetns(t *testing.T, pid int, url string) metrics.Exposition {
479		t.Helper()
480		nsPath := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/ns/net", pid)
481	
482		dialInNetns := func(ctx context.Context, network, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
483			type result struct {
484				conn net.Conn
485				err  error
486			}
487			done := make(chan result, 1)
488			go func() {
489				runtime.LockOSThread() // deliberately never unlocked: goroutine exit kills the thread
490	
491				f, err := os.Open(nsPath)
492				if err != nil {
493					done <- result{err: fmt.Errorf("open %s: %w", nsPath, err)}
494					return
495				}
496				defer f.Close()
497				if err := unix.Setns(int(f.Fd()), unix.CLONE_NEWNET); err != nil {
498					done <- result{err: fmt.Errorf("setns into concentrator netns: %w", err)}
499					return
500				}
501				// The socket() syscall runs HERE, on this locked, peer-netns thread, so the
502				// connection is opened inside the concentrator's namespace. addr is a loopback
503				// IP literal (no DNS, single address), so net.Dialer opens exactly one socket on
504				// this goroutine — no background resolver/happy-eyeballs goroutine escapes it.
505				var d net.Dialer
506				c, err := d.DialContext(ctx, network, addr)
507				done <- result{conn: c, err: err}
508			}()
509			r := <-done
510			return r.conn, r.err
511		}
512	
513		client := &http.Client{Transport: &http.Transport{DisableKeepAlives: true, DialContext: dialInNetns}}
514		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
515		defer cancel()
516		exp, err := metrics.Fetch(ctx, client, url)
517		if err != nil {
518			t.Fatalf("scrape concentrator %s in netns: %v", url, err)
519		}
520		return exp
521	}
522	
523	// p2Path returns the named DefaultPaths spec with the P2 per-path rate cap applied.
524	func p2Path(name string) pathSpec {
525		for _, p := range DefaultPaths {
526			if p.name == name {
527				p.rateMbit = p2RateMbit
528				return p
529			}
530		}
531		panic("p2Path: unknown path " + name)
532	}
533	
534	// p2Paths returns both DefaultPaths specs with the P2 per-path rate cap applied.
535	func p2Paths() []pathSpec {
536		return []pathSpec{p2Path("starlink"), p2Path("cellular")}
537	}
538	
539	// setupP2Tunnel brings up the edge+concentrator tunnel over paths with the WEIGHTED
540	// scheduler (so aggregation engages under load) and the /metrics endpoint enabled on both
541	// ends. It mirrors setupMultipathTunnel's addressing/bring-up but adds the [scheduler] and
542	// [metrics] config blocks T23 needs. perPathCapacityFPS sizes the weighted aggregation gate:
543	// the ratio/thrift paths pass p2PerPathCapacityFPS; the functional striping subtest passes
544	// the low p2StripingCapacityFPS so aggregation reliably engages on the CPU/PPS-bound fixture.
545	func setupP2Tunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, perPathCapacityFPS float64) (edge, conc *proc) {
546		t.Helper()
547	
548		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
549		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
550		psk := randKey(t)
551	
552		var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
553		for _, p := range paths {
554			fmt.Fprintf(&edgePaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
555			fmt.Fprintf(&concPaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
556		}
557		primary := paths[0]
558	
559		// The weighted scheduler with an aggregation gate sized by the caller (perPathCapacityFPS).
560		// Pacing is left OFF so the LINKS, not a token bucket, are the throughput bottleneck; the
561		// gate alone engages/collapses aggregation.
562		schedBlock := fmt.Sprintf("[scheduler]\npolicy = \"weighted\"\nper_path_capacity_fps = %.1f\n\n", perPathCapacityFPS)
563		metricsBlock := fmt.Sprintf("[metrics]\nlisten = %q\n\n", p2MetricsListen)
564	
565		dir := t.TempDir()
566		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
567	psk = "%s"
568	
569	%s%s%s[wireguard]
570	private_key = "%s"
571	
572	[[wireguard.peers]]
573	public_key = "%s"
574	endpoint = "%s:%d"
575	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
576	
577	[log]
578	level = "error"
579	`, psk, edgePaths.String(), schedBlock, metricsBlock, edgePriv, concPub, primary.concIP, listenPort, concInner))
580	
581		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
582	psk = "%s"
583	
584	%s%s%s[wireguard]
585	private_key = "%s"
586	listen_port = %d
587	
588	[[wireguard.peers]]
589	public_key = "%s"
590	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
591	
592	[log]
593	level = "error"
594	`, psk, concPaths.String(), schedBlock, metricsBlock, concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner))
595	
596		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
597		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
598	
599		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
600			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
601		}
602		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
603			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
604		}
605		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
606		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
607		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
608		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
609		return edge, conc
610	}

2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"bytes"
7		"crypto/ecdh"
8		"crypto/rand"
9		"encoding/base64"
10		"encoding/json"
11		"fmt"
12		"os"
13		"os/exec"

15		"strconv"
16		"sync"
17		"syscall"
18		"testing"
19		"time"

21	
22	// P0 overlay addressing: the inner /24 carried by the WireGuard TUN on both ends.
23	const (
24		edgeInner  = "10.10.0.1"
25		concInner  = "10.10.0.2"
26		tunDev     = "wanbond0"
27		listenPort = 51820
28	)
29	
30	// TestP0PassThrough is the P0 acceptance gate: bring the wanbond tunnel up over a
31	// single path (no bonding) with the pass-through Bind, complete the WireGuard
32	// handshake, and verify both ICMP (ping) and a TCP bulk transfer (iperf3) flow
33	// through the tunnel between the edge and concentrator network namespaces.
34	func TestP0PassThrough(t *testing.T) {
35		top := Setup(t)
36		p := top.path("starlink")
37		bin := buildWanbond(t)
38	
39		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
40		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
41		psk := randKey(t) // outer-control PSK: required by config validation, unused by P0 pass-through
42	
43		dir := t.TempDir()
44		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
45	psk = "%s"
46	
47	[[paths]]
48	name = "starlink"
49	source_addr = "%s"
50	
51	[wireguard]
52	private_key = "%s"
53	
54	[[wireguard.peers]]
55	public_key = "%s"
56	endpoint = "%s:%d"
57	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
58	
59	[log]
60	level = "error"
61	`, psk, p.edgeIP, edgePriv, concPub, p.concIP, listenPort, concInner))
62	
63		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
64	psk = "%s"
65	
66	[[paths]]
67	name = "starlink"
68	source_addr = "%s"
69	
70	[wireguard]
71	private_key = "%s"
72	listen_port = %d
73	
74	[[wireguard.peers]]
75	public_key = "%s"
76	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
77	
78	[log]
79	level = "error"
80	`, psk, p.concIP, concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner))
81	
82		// Concentrator first (it must be listening before the edge initiates), then edge.
83		conc := top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
84		edge := top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
85	
86		// Wait for each side's TUN, then address it (wanbond owns the engine only;
87		// addressing is the operator's job — here, the test's).
88		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
89			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
90		}
91		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
92			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
93		}
94		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
95		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
96		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
97		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
98	
99		// The first ping triggers the handshake; retry until the session is established.
100		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
101			t.Fatalf("tunnel never came up: %s unreachable through the tunnel\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- concentrator ---\n%s",
102				concInner, edge.log(), conc.log())
103		}
104	
105		mbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3)
106		if mbps <= 0 {
107			t.Fatalf("iperf3 through the tunnel measured non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", mbps)
108		}
109		t.Logf("P0 tunnel up: handshake + ping OK, iperf3 = %.1f Mbit/s", mbps)
110	}
111	
112	// buildWanbond compiles the daemon (plain build, no e2e tag) into a temp path and
113	// returns it. The build uses the module-qualified package path so it resolves
114	// regardless of the test's working directory.
115	func buildWanbond(t *testing.T) string {
116		t.Helper()
117		bin := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "wanbond")
118		out, err := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", bin, "github.com/7mind/wanbond/cmd/wanbond").CombinedOutput()
119		if err != nil {
120			t.Fatalf("build wanbond: %v\n%s", err, out)
121		}
122		return bin
123	}
124	
125	// genKey generates an X25519 keypair (the WireGuard key format) using the
126	// standard library, returning both keys base64-encoded as the TOML config
127	// carries them.
128	func genKey(t *testing.T) (privB64, pubB64 string) {
129		t.Helper()
130		priv, err := ecdh.X25519().GenerateKey(rand.Reader)
131		if err != nil {
132			t.Fatalf("generate X25519 key: %v", err)
133		}
134		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.Bytes()),
135			base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.PublicKey().Bytes())
136	}
137	
138	// randKey returns 32 random bytes base64-encoded (a placeholder PSK).
139	func randKey(t *testing.T) string {
140		t.Helper()
141		var b [32]byte
142		if _, err := rand.Read(b[:]); err != nil {
143			t.Fatalf("read random: %v", err)
144		}
145		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(b[:])
146	}
147	
148	// writeConfig writes body to path with the exact 0600 mode config.Load requires.
149	func writeConfig(t *testing.T, path, body string) string {
150		t.Helper()
151		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(body), 0o600); err != nil {
152			t.Fatalf("write config %s: %v", path, err)
153		}
154		if err := os.Chmod(path, 0o600); err != nil { // defeat umask widening
155			t.Fatalf("chmod config %s: %v", path, err)
156		}
157		return path
158	}
159	
160	// lockedBuffer is a bytes.Buffer safe for concurrent Write (by exec.Cmd's output
161	// copier goroutine) and String (by the test's diagnostic log() calls).
162	type lockedBuffer struct {
163		mu  sync.Mutex
164		buf bytes.Buffer
165	}
166	
167	func (b *lockedBuffer) Write(p []byte) (int, error) {
168		b.mu.Lock()
169		defer b.mu.Unlock()
170		return b.buf.Write(p)
171	}
172	
173	func (b *lockedBuffer) String() string {
174		b.mu.Lock()
175		defer b.mu.Unlock()
176		return b.buf.String()
177	}
178	
179	// proc is a background process with captured combined output.
180	type proc struct {
181		cmd    *exec.Cmd
182		name   string
183		output *lockedBuffer
184	}
185	
186	func (p *proc) log() string { return p.output.String() }
187	
188	// startProc launches argv in the background, capturing its combined output and
189	// registering a cleanup that terminates it (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after a grace
190	// period). A concentrator process is launched via nsenter so it runs — and
191	// creates its TUN — inside the peer network namespace.
192	func (top *Topology) startProc(t *testing.T, name string, argv ...string) *proc {
193		t.Helper()
194		cmd := exec.Command(argv[0], argv[1:]...)
195		out := &lockedBuffer{}
196		cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = out, out
197		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
198			t.Fatalf("start %s (%v): %v", name, argv, err)
199		}
200		p := &proc{cmd: cmd, name: name, output: out}
201		t.Cleanup(func() {
202			if cmd.Process == nil {
203				return
204			}
205			_ = cmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGTERM)
206			done := make(chan struct{})
207			// cmd.Wait (not Process.Wait) reaps the child AND waits for the output
208			// copier goroutines, so no write races the final log() read.
209			go func() { _ = cmd.Wait(); close(done) }()
210			select {
211			case <-done:
212			case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
213				_ = cmd.Process.Kill()
214				<-done
215			}
216		})
217		return p
218	}
219	
220	// waitLink polls until dev exists (in the peer netns when ns is true), up to d.
221	func (top *Topology) waitLink(dev string, ns bool, d time.Duration) bool {
222		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
223		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
224			var err error
225			if ns {
226				err = top.tryRun("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ip", "link", "show", dev)
227			} else {
228				err = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "show", dev)
229			}
230			if err == nil {
231				return true
232			}
233			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
234		}
235		return false
236	}
237	
238	// pingUntil pings ip from the edge netns until it answers or d elapses. The first
239	// successful reply implies the WireGuard session is established.
240	func (top *Topology) pingUntil(ip string, d time.Duration) bool {
241		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
242		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
243			if top.tryRun("ping", "-c", "1", "-W", "1", ip) == nil {
244				return true
245			}
246			time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
247		}
248		return false
249	}
250	
251	// iperfDefaultPort is iperf3's default server port (5201), used by iperf3Mbps,
252	// which starts its server without an explicit -p. The under-load measurement uses
253	// the distinct bloatPort instead.
254	const iperfDefaultPort = 5201
255	
256	// iperf3Mbps runs a one-shot iperf3 TCP transfer to serverIP (inside the peer
257	// netns) for secs seconds and returns the sender throughput in Mbit/s.
258	func (top *Topology) iperf3Mbps(t *testing.T, serverIP string, secs int) float64 {
259		t.Helper()
260		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", serverIP)
261		top.waitIperfListen(t, iperfDefaultPort) // poll for LISTEN (never connect: server is one-shot)
262	
263		out := top.runOut("iperf3", "-c", serverIP, "-t", strconv.Itoa(secs), "-J")
264		var r struct {
265			End struct {
266				SumSent struct {
267					BitsPerSecond float64 `json:"bits_per_second"`
268				} `json:"sum_sent"`
269			} `json:"end"`
270		}
271		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &r); err != nil {
272			t.Fatalf("parse iperf3 json: %v\n%s", err, out)
273		}
274		return r.End.SumSent.BitsPerSecond / 1e6
275	}

3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"

15	)
16	
17	// TestMultipathPerPath is the T12 per-path acceptance: the multipath Bind carries
18	// tunnel traffic over EACH configured path individually. Each subtest brings the
19	// bond up with exactly ONE path configured (the other "disabled" by omission) and
20	// verifies the WireGuard handshake completes and both ICMP and a TCP bulk transfer
21	// flow over that single path's source-bound socket + outer DATA framing.
22	func TestMultipathPerPath(t *testing.T) {
23		bin := buildWanbond(t)
24		for _, p := range DefaultPaths {
25			p := p
26			t.Run(p.name, func(t *testing.T) {
27				top := Setup(t)
28				edge, conc := setupMultipathTunnel(t, top, bin, []pathSpec{p})
29	
30				if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
31					t.Fatalf("path %q: tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
32						p.name, edge.log(), conc.log())
33				}
34				mbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3)
35				if mbps <= 0 {
36					t.Fatalf("path %q: iperf3 non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", p.name, mbps)
37				}
38				t.Logf("multipath single-path [%s]: handshake + ping OK, iperf3 = %.1f Mbit/s", p.name, mbps)
39			})
40		}
41	}
42	
43	// TestMultipathBothPathsAndFailover brings the bond up with BOTH paths configured,
44	// confirms the tunnel is up, then blackholes the secondary path and confirms the
45	// tunnel still carries traffic over the primary — demonstrating each path is an
46	// independent socket beneath the one virtual endpoint (the engine sees no churn).
47	// (Runtime scheduling/failover across paths is T15; T12's policy is "first healthy
48	// path", so the primary continues to carry traffic while the secondary is down.)
49	func TestMultipathBothPathsAndFailover(t *testing.T) {
50		bin := buildWanbond(t)
51		top := Setup(t)
52		edge, conc := setupMultipathTunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths)
53	
54		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
55			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
56		}
57	
58		// Blackhole the secondary path; the primary (first configured) still carries
59		// traffic.
60		top.Blackhole(DefaultPaths[1].name)
61		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 10*time.Second) {
62			t.Fatalf("bond lost connectivity after blackholing secondary path %q\n--- edge ---\n%s",
63				DefaultPaths[1].name, edge.log())
64		}
65		top.Restore(DefaultPaths[1].name)
66		t.Logf("multipath both-paths: up over %d paths; survived blackhole of %q", len(DefaultPaths), DefaultPaths[1].name)
67	}
68	
69	// TestMultipathInnerMTU asserts the TUN MTU the daemon set equals the computed
70	// inner MTU = path MTU − (outer IP/UDP + outer DATA frame + WG overhead), the
71	// fixture value pinned by the bind package's unit test.
72	func TestMultipathInnerMTU(t *testing.T) {
73		bin := buildWanbond(t)
74		top := Setup(t)
75		setupMultipathTunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths)
76	
77		// This fixture runs the daemon with FEC OFF (DefaultPaths carries no [fec] block),
78		// so the TUN MTU is the FEC-off inner budget.
79		want := bind.InnerMTU(bind.DefaultPathMTU, false)
80		got := top.linkMTU(t, tunDev, false)
81		if got != want {
82			t.Fatalf("edge %s MTU = %d, want computed inner MTU %d", tunDev, got, want)
83		}
84		t.Logf("multipath inner MTU: %s MTU = %d (= %d path MTU − %d IP/UDP − %d DATA frame − %d WG)",
85			tunDev, got, bind.DefaultPathMTU, bind.IPv4UDPOverhead, 40, bind.WGTransportOverhead)
86	}
87	
88	// TestMultipathNoFragmentation sends a max-inner-MTU payload with the DF bit set
89	// through the tunnel and asserts, from a capture on the edge egress veth, that no
90	// outer datagram is IP-fragmented — the MTU accounting keeps a full inner packet
91	// inside the path MTU (no fragmentation, no PMTUD black hole).
92	func TestMultipathNoFragmentation(t *testing.T) {
93		bin := buildWanbond(t)
94		top := Setup(t)
95		p := top.path("starlink")
96		edge, _ := setupMultipathTunnel(t, top, bin, []pathSpec{p})
97	
98		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
99			t.Fatalf("tunnel never came up\n%s", edge.log())
100		}
101	
102		inner := bind.InnerMTU(bind.DefaultPathMTU, false)
103		// ICMP echo payload that exactly fills the inner MTU: inner − 20 (IP) − 8 (ICMP).
104		payload := inner - 28
105	
106		// Capture fragments on the edge egress veth while a DF-set, max-size ping runs.
107		frags := top.captureFragments(t, p.edgeVeth, 6*time.Second, func() {
108			out, err := exec.Command("ping", "-c", "5", "-s", strconv.Itoa(payload), "-M", "do", "-W", "2", concInner).CombinedOutput()
109			if err != nil {
110				t.Fatalf("DF max-MTU ping (payload %d) failed — inner packet does not fit the budget:\n%s", payload, out)
111			}
112		})
113		if frags != 0 {
114			t.Fatalf("observed %d IP-fragmented outer datagrams on %s during a max-inner-MTU transfer; MTU accounting is wrong", frags, p.edgeVeth)
115		}
116		t.Logf("multipath no-fragmentation: %d-byte inner packets (payload %d) produced no IP fragments on %s", inner, payload, p.edgeVeth)
117	}
118	
119	// setupMultipathTunnel brings the multipath tunnel up over the given paths: the
120	// edge binds one source-addr'd socket per path and targets each path's
121	// concentrator address via dest_addr; the concentrator binds one socket per path
122	// on its own address. It addresses both TUNs and returns the running processes.
123	func setupMultipathTunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec) (edge, conc *proc) {
124		t.Helper()
125		return setupMultipathTunnelLevel(t, top, bin, paths, "error")
126	}
127	
128	// setupMultipathTunnelLevel is setupMultipathTunnel with an explicit daemon log
129	// level. The P1 failover measurement (TestP1Failover) runs both daemons at "info"
130	// so the scheduler's per-direction "active path change" transitions are captured in
131	// each process's log for the root-cause evidence; every other caller uses "error".
132	func setupMultipathTunnelLevel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, level string) (edge, conc *proc) {
133		t.Helper()
134	
135		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
136		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
137		psk := randKey(t)
138	
139		var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
140		for _, p := range paths {
141			fmt.Fprintf(&edgePaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
142			fmt.Fprintf(&concPaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
143		}
144		// The wireguard peer endpoint (edge -> concentrator) seeds the virtual
145		// endpoint; the primary path's concentrator address serves.
146		primary := paths[0]
147	
148		dir := t.TempDir()
149		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
150	psk = "%s"
151	
152	%s[wireguard]
153	private_key = "%s"
154	
155	[[wireguard.peers]]
156	public_key = "%s"
157	endpoint = "%s:%d"
158	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
159	
160	[log]
161	level = %q
162	`, psk, edgePaths.String(), edgePriv, concPub, primary.concIP, listenPort, concInner, level))
163	
164		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
165	psk = "%s"
166	
167	%s[wireguard]
168	private_key = "%s"
169	listen_port = %d
170	
171	[[wireguard.peers]]
172	public_key = "%s"
173	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
174	
175	[log]
176	level = %q
177	`, psk, concPaths.String(), concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner, level))
178	
179		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
180		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
181	
182		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
183			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
184		}
185		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
186			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
187		}
188		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
189		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
190		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
191		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
192		return edge, conc
193	}
194	
195	// linkMTU returns the MTU of dev (in the peer netns when ns is true).
196	func (top *Topology) linkMTU(t *testing.T, dev string, ns bool) int {
197		t.Helper()
198		var out string
199		if ns {
200			out = top.runOut("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ip", "-o", "link", "show", dev)
201		} else {
202			out = top.runOut("ip", "-o", "link", "show", dev)
203		}
204		// ... mtu 1401 ...
205		fields := strings.Fields(out)
206		for i, f := range fields {
207			if f == "mtu" && i+1 < len(fields) {
208				mtu, err := strconv.Atoi(fields[i+1])
209				if err != nil {
210					t.Fatalf("parse mtu %q: %v", fields[i+1], err)
211				}
212				return mtu
213			}
214		}
215		t.Fatalf("no mtu field in %q", out)
216		return 0
217	}
218	
219	// captureFragments runs tcpdump on veth for the duration of during(), counting
220	// IP-fragmented datagrams (tcpdump annotates them "(frag ...)").
221	func (top *Topology) captureFragments(t *testing.T, veth string, d time.Duration, during func()) int {
222		t.Helper()
223		secs := int(d.Seconds())
224		if secs < 1 {
225			secs = 1
226		}
227		cap := exec.Command("timeout", strconv.Itoa(secs), "tcpdump", "-n", "-l", "-i", veth)
228		out := &lockedBuffer{}
229		cap.Stdout, cap.Stderr = out, out
230		if err := cap.Start(); err != nil {
231			t.Fatalf("start tcpdump on %s: %v", veth, err)
232		}
233		time.Sleep(700 * time.Millisecond) // let tcpdump attach before generating traffic
234		during()
235		time.Sleep(300 * time.Millisecond) // let trailing packets flush to the capture
236		_ = cap.Wait()                     // timeout terminates it
237	
238		count := 0
239		for _, line := range strings.Split(out.String(), "\n") {
240			if strings.Contains(line, "frag ") {
241				count++
242			}
243		}
244		return count
245	}

1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
9	)
10	
11	// Acceptance thresholds (Q1). These are the single source of truth for the
12	// per-phase verification criteria; every e2e assertion reads them from here so
13	// retuning after real-link measurements is a one-line change. Values are the
14	// approved Q1 defaults.
15	const (
16		// P1RecoverySeconds bounds transparent-failover recovery: after the active
17		// WAN is killed, the surviving TCP flow must have throughput restored within
18		// this many seconds, with no connection reset.
19		P1RecoverySeconds = 3
20	
21		// P2BondedMinFraction is the minimum bonded throughput as a fraction of the
22		// sum of the two paths' individual throughputs, under saturating load.
23		P2BondedMinFraction = 0.85
24		// P2MeteredMaxByteFraction caps the metered (5G) path's share of total bytes
25		// while the primary (Starlink) path is healthy — data-thrift.
26		P2MeteredMaxByteFraction = 0.01
27	
28		// P3MinRecoveredFraction is the minimum fraction of lost DATA frames the RS
29		// FEC must recover without retransmit, at each injected loss rate.
30		P3MinRecoveredFraction = 0.95
31		// P3MaxOverheadFactor bounds measured FEC overhead as a multiple of the
32		// configured parity ratio.
33		P3MaxOverheadFactor = 2.0
34	
35		// P4ResidualLossMax is the maximum post-recovery residual loss under steady
36		// P4SteadyLossRate path loss with adaptive FEC engaged.
37		P4ResidualLossMax = 0.005
38		// P4SteadyLossRate is the steady per-path loss rate at which the P4 residual
39		// and overhead-vs-baseline comparisons are made.
40		P4SteadyLossRate = 0.05
41	)
42	
43	// P3InjectedLossRates are the uniform loss rates at which fixed-ratio FEC
44	// recovery and overhead are asserted (P3).
45	var P3InjectedLossRates = []float64{0.05, 0.15}
46	
47	// P4 adaptive-overhead acceptance is a comparison, not a scalar: for equal loss
48	// masking, adaptive total overhead bytes must be <= the P3 fixed-FEC baseline
49	// bytes. The comparison is performed in the P4 e2e test against a recorded
50	// baseline run rather than a constant here.
51	
52	// Per-path liveness detection (T13/T39). Path liveness is entirely ours — the inner
53	// WireGuard keepalive is per-peer, not per-path — so a blackholed uplink under a
54	// live peer is detected only by these probes. These are NOT independent constants:
55	// they are ALIASES of the daemon's telemetry.Default* timing, so the e2e asserts
56	// against the exact cadence the daemon runs. This is the D16 reconciliation — there
57	// is one source of truth (internal/telemetry), not two tables that drift apart.
58	// PLivenessDownAfter is the silence (no authenticated probe echo) after which an up
59	// path is marked down; PLivenessProbeInterval is the probe/Tick cadence; detection
60	// latency is therefore bounded by DownAfter plus roughly one interval, and the e2e
61	// asserts a blackholed path is marked down within PLivenessDetectBudget.
62	//
63	// Relationship to P1RecoverySeconds (the 3s failover-recovery budget). Killing the
64	// ACTIVE WAN must restore the surviving flow within P1RecoverySeconds in BOTH
65	// directions. Recovery in EACH direction is (that side marks the path down) +
66	// (its scheduler reroutes, a sub-ms data-structure update). Crucially, failover is
67	// BIDIRECTIONAL and the two ends detect INDEPENDENTLY: the edge must mark the path
68	// down to reroute egress, AND the concentrator must mark it down to reroute its
69	// replies (D15/D16 — the earlier note budgeted only the edge term). Both detection
70	// clocks start at the kill and run CONCURRENTLY, so end-to-end bidirectional
71	// recovery is governed by the SLOWER of the two ends, not their sum:
72	//
73	//	recovery ≈ max(edgeDetect, concDetect) + reroute
74	//	         ≈ (DownAfter + one interval) + reroute        [the two ends are symmetric]
75	//	         = 1.2s + 0.2s + sub-ms ≈ 1.4s                 [worst-case detect, strict-'>' Tick]
76	//	         < PLivenessFailoverBudget = 1.6s              [budget adds one interval of headroom]
77	//
78	// So the NO-LOAD analytical recovery FLOOR is the detect term, DownAfter + one
79	// interval = 1.4s (the strict-'>' Tick needs one full interval past DownAfter to
80	// fire) plus a sub-ms reroute. PLivenessFailoverBudget = DownAfter + TWO intervals =
81	// 1.6s deliberately carries ONE interval BEYOND that 1.4s detect as headroom — it is
82	// a bound with slack, not the detect floor itself. The remaining ~1.4s up to the 3s
83	// deadline (3.0s − 1.6s) is the margin for CPU-scheduling jitter under a saturating
84	// flow — the D15 tail. Two T39 changes
85	// bought that margin back after the tail was found at ~3.1s: DownAfter was tightened
86	// 1500ms→1200ms and the interval 250ms→200ms (detect floor 1.75s→1.4s) WITHOUT reducing the
87	// six-lost-echo false-down tolerance (the ratio is unchanged); and the bind now
88	// advances liveness off the always-scheduled receive path too (D15), so a starved
89	// probe-loop timer no longer delays the concentrator-side detect. The correctness
90	// invariant when retuning is PLivenessFailoverBudget < P1RecoverySeconds, and the
91	// operational target is that MEASURED recovery under saturating load stays below
92	// P1RecoverySeconds every run — TestP1Failover is the guard.
93	//
94	// NOTE PLivenessDetectBudget is deliberately LARGER than PLivenessFailoverBudget: it
95	// is the SINGLE-PATH blackhole ASSERTION DEADLINE for TestLivenessBlackhole (detect
96	// worst-case + harness slack so that pump-loop e2e is not flaky), NOT the
97	// failover-composition term.
98	const (
99		PLivenessProbeInterval = telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval
100		PLivenessDownAfter     = telemetry.DefaultDownAfter
101		PLivenessUpSuccesses   = telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses
102		// PLivenessFailoverBudget is the analytical per-direction failover-recovery bound:
103		// the 1.4s worst-case detect (DownAfter + one interval, strict-'>' Tick) plus one
104		// interval of headroom, = DownAfter + 2 intervals = 1.6s (the sub-ms reroute is
105		// absorbed by that headroom). Both directions are symmetric and detect concurrently,
106		// so it bounds end-to-end BIDIRECTIONAL recovery. It MUST stay below P1RecoverySeconds
107		// (1.6s < 3s, a ~1.4s jitter margin); TestP1Failover asserts measured recovery against
108		// P1RecoverySeconds and reports its margin against this budget.
109		PLivenessFailoverBudget = PLivenessDownAfter + 2*PLivenessProbeInterval
110		// PLivenessDetectBudget is the single-path blackhole assertion deadline (harness
111		// slack added on top of the analytical detect), NOT the failover budget.
112		PLivenessDetectBudget = PLivenessDownAfter + 1500*time.Millisecond
113	)

1420		ps := peer.paths[idx]
1421		remote, ok := ps.getRemote()
1422		if !ok {
1423			m.mu.Unlock()
1424			return errNoHealthyPath
1425		}
1426		c := ps.conn
1427		wires := make([]fecWire, 0, len(bufs))
1428		for _, b := range bufs {
1429			seq := peer.outerSeq.Add(1)
1430			if peer.fecSend != nil {
1431				// FEC on (T24): admit the inner datagram (coded as seq || inner) to the group
1432				// encoder. The returned data shard rides a normal DATA frame carrying its FEC
1433				// group + shard index; when this admission FILLS the group the encoder returns
1434				// the group's parity shards, emitted here as KindParity frames on the SAME
1435				// chosen path. Spreading parity onto a DIFFERENT path than its data (so one
1436				// path outage cannot lose both) is a documented future refinement, deliberately
1437				// NOT implemented here (see the T24 design notes).
1438				ds, parity, err := peer.fecSend.enc.Admit(fecShardPayload(seq, b))
1439				if err != nil {
1440					m.mu.Unlock()
1441					return err
1442				}
1443				wire, err := peer.sendCodec.Encode(nil, frame.Data{OuterSeq: seq, PathID: ps.id, FECGroup: uint32(ds.Group), FECIndex: uint8(ds.Index), Payload: b})
1444				if err != nil {
1445					m.mu.Unlock()
1446					return err
1447				}
1448				wires = append(wires, fecWire{b: wire})
1449				for _, par := range parity {
1450					pw, err := m.encodeParityLocked(peer, par, ps.id)
1451					if err != nil {
1452						m.mu.Unlock()
1453						return err
1454					}
1455					wires = append(wires, fecWire{b: pw, parity: true})
1456				}
1457				continue
1458			}
1459			wire, err := peer.sendCodec.Encode(nil, frame.Data{OuterSeq: seq, PathID: ps.id, Payload: b})
1460			if err != nil {
1461				m.mu.Unlock()
1462				return err
1463			}
1464			wires = append(wires, fecWire{b: wire})
1465		}
1466		fs := peer.fecSend
1467		m.mu.Unlock()
1468	
1469		for _, w := range wires {
1470			if _, err := c.WriteToUDPAddrPort(w.b, remote); err != nil {
1471				return err
1472			}
1473			// Per-path egress-wire accounting (T23): count the OUTER frame bytes just written
1474			// to this path (DATA and any FEC PARITY alike), lock-free and only for a datagram
1475			// that actually reached the socket. Send serialized the path choice under m.mu, so
1476			// ps is fixed here and this is the sole writer of ps.txBytes for this frame.
1477			ps.txBytes.Add(uint64(len(w.b)))
1478			if fs != nil {
1479				// FEC frame accounting (T24/T25), counted only once the frame reached the socket
1480				// so the /metrics overhead ratio reflects wire cost actually spent. Parity and
1481				// DATA are charged to disjoint counters; their ratio ParityFrames/DataFrames is
1482				// the fixed-ratio overhead the P3 e2e asserts against M/K. fs != nil only when FEC
1483				// is enabled, so a plain (FEC-off) datapath increments neither counter.
1484				if w.parity {
1485					fs.parityFrames.Add(1)
1486					fs.parityBytes.Add(uint64(len(w.b)))
1487				} else {
1488					fs.dataFrames.Add(1)
1489					// DATA-frame wire bytes: the overhead-BYTES denominator (T29). The P4
1490					// acceptance compares parity BYTES / data BYTES, so both are counted on the
1491					// same only-once-it-reached-the-socket basis as the frame counters.
1492					fs.dataBytes.Add(uint64(len(w.b)))
1493				}
1494			}
1495		}
1496		return nil
1497	}
1498	
1499	// fecWire is one framed outgoing datagram tagged with whether it is an FEC parity
1500	// frame, so the write loop can charge parity-overhead accounting (T24) without a
1501	// second pass.
1502	type fecWire struct {
1503		b      []byte
1504		parity bool
1505	}
1506	
1507	// encodeParityLocked encodes one parity shard as a KindParity frame on the given
1508	// path, using the owning peer's send Codec. Caller holds m.mu (the send Codec is
1509	// shared and stateful per peer).
1510	func (m *Multipath) encodeParityLocked(peer *peerState, par fec.ParityShard, pathID uint8) ([]byte, error) {
1511		return peer.sendCodec.Encode(nil, frame.Parity{
1512			FECGroup:    uint32(par.Group),
1513			ParityIndex: uint16(par.Index),
1514			DataCount:   uint8(par.DataCount),
1515			PathID:      pathID,
1516			Payload:     par.Payload,
1517		})
1518	}
1519	
1520	// fecTickLoop drives the FEC encoder's grouping deadline (T24): a partially-filled
1521	// group whose size threshold has not been reached is closed on time so its parity is
1522	// emitted rather than stranded until the next data frame. It ticks at the configured
1523	// deadline period and exits on recvClosed. It is tracked by readersWG so Close waits
1524	// for it. Like tickLivenessFromReceive it only ever TryLocks m.mu, so it can never
1525	// deadlock Close's readersWG.Wait (which runs while Close holds m.mu).
1526	func (m *Multipath) fecTickLoop(period time.Duration, closed <-chan struct{}) {
1527		defer m.readersWG.Done()
1528		ticker := time.NewTicker(period)
1529		defer ticker.Stop()
1530		for {
1531			select {
1532			case <-closed:
1533				return
1534			case <-ticker.C:
1535				m.fecFlushDeadline()
1536			}
1537		}
1538	}
1539	
1540	// fecFlushDeadline closes any FEC group whose grouping deadline has elapsed and emits
1541	// its parity on a scheduler-chosen path. It TryLocks m.mu: when the lock is contended
1542	// (a concurrent Send/Close/AddPath) it simply skips this tick — the next tick, or the
1543	// next size-triggered close, still emits the group's parity, and skipping preserves
1544	// Close's invariant that no readersWG goroutine blocks on m.mu. Framing runs under the
1545	// lock (the shared stateful send Codec); the socket writes run without it, mirroring
1546	// Send.
1547	func (m *Multipath) fecFlushDeadline() {
1548		if !m.mu.TryLock() {
1549			return
1550		}
1551		if m.fecSend == nil || len(m.paths) == 0 {
1552			m.mu.Unlock()
1553			return
1554		}
1555		// Adaptive drive (T29): fold a fresh loss sample into the controller and retarget the
1556		// encoder's per-group parity BEFORE flushing, so a group closed by this tick already
1557		// carries the current target. It is a no-op in fixed mode and self-throttles to the
1558		// probe cadence. Runs under the same m.mu the flush holds — the single serialized FEC
1559		// locus — so the controller (a state machine) is never touched concurrently.
1560		m.driveAdaptiveControllerLocked()
1561		parity, err := m.fecSend.enc.Tick()
1562		if err != nil || len(parity) == 0 {
1563			m.mu.Unlock()
1564			return
1565		}
1566		// Route the parity like data through the scheduler (a first-integration choice; see
1567		// Send). A shed/no-path verdict drops this group's parity — a degraded path is
1568		// exactly when the datapath is under pressure, and a stranded partial group's parity
1569		// is best-effort — so the group simply goes unprotected rather than blocking. FEC
1570		// parity is bulk redundancy, so it is paced as ClassData (defect D22): only WireGuard
1571		// control frames earn the pacing exemption.
1572		idx := m.scheduler.Pick(sched.ClassData)
1573		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.paths) {
1574			m.mu.Unlock()
1575			return
1576		}
1577		ps := m.paths[idx]
1578		remote, ok := ps.getRemote()
1579		if !ok {
1580			m.mu.Unlock()
1581			return
1582		}
1583		c := ps.conn
1584		fs := m.fecSend
1585		wires := make([][]byte, 0, len(parity))
1586		for _, par := range parity {
1587			// fecFlushDeadline drives the PRIMARY peer's FEC group (m.fecSend/m.scheduler/
1588			// m.paths reach the primary by promotion), so its parity is framed with the
1589			// primary's send Codec.
1590			pw, err := m.encodeParityLocked(m.peerState, par, ps.id)
1591			if err != nil {
1592				m.mu.Unlock()
1593				return
1594			}
1595			wires = append(wires, pw)
1596		}
1597		m.mu.Unlock()
1598	
1599		for _, wire := range wires {
1600			if _, err := c.WriteToUDPAddrPort(wire, remote); err != nil {
1601				return
1602			}
1603			ps.txBytes.Add(uint64(len(wire)))
1604			fs.parityFrames.Add(1)
1605			fs.parityBytes.Add(uint64(len(wire)))
1606		}
1607	}
1608	
1609	// driveAdaptiveControllerLocked folds one measured loss sample into the adaptive FEC
1610	// controller and retargets the encoder's per-group parity (T29). Caller holds m.mu, so
1611	// the controller — a state machine that is NOT safe for concurrent use — is driven from
1612	// this single serialized locus (the FEC tick loop), exactly as the encoder is. It is a
1613	// no-op in fixed-ratio mode (no controller) and self-throttles to adaptiveControlInterval
1614	// so the controller's EWMA sees ~one sample per probe interval regardless of the tick rate.
1615	//
1616	// WHICH LOSS drives the controller (design decision 1): the MAX raw probe-measured loss
1617	// (Estimate().Loss) across the currently-eligible (StateUp) paths. Parity must mask the
1618	// loss the DATA actually experiences; under active-backup only the primary carries data
1619	// and the max collapses to it, and under the weighted scheduler data is striped across the
1620	// eligible set, so sizing to the worst active path is the defensible, policy-agnostic
1621	// choice. It is deliberately the RAW per-path loss, NOT the post-recovery ConnLoss: feeding
1622	// the masked residual back would form a control loop that under-provisions precisely because
1623	// it is succeeding. A down/probeless path is excluded — it carries no data to protect.
1624	func (m *Multipath) driveAdaptiveControllerLocked() {
1625		fs := m.fecSend
1626		if fs.ctrl == nil {
1627			return // fixed-ratio mode
1628		}
1629		now := time.Now()
1630		if fs.haveControlTick && now.Sub(fs.lastControlTick) < adaptiveControlInterval {
1631			return
1632		}
1633		fs.lastControlTick = now
1634		fs.haveControlTick = true
1635	
1636		loss, have := m.maxEligiblePathLossLocked()
1637		if !have {
1638			return // no eligible probed path this interval: hold the current target
1639		}

1	package bind
2	
3	import (
4		"sync"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
8	)
9	
10	// emitProbes performs one probe cadence step: for every currently-open path it
11	// emits one authenticated PROBE frame (IsEcho=false) to that path's learned/
12	// configured remote, then Ticks that path's Prober so liveness advances against
13	// the injected clock. A path without a known remote yet is still Ticked (so a
14	// silent path is detected Down) but nothing is sent — there is nowhere to send.
15	//
16	// Concurrency mirrors Send: the path/prober set is snapshotted under m.mu, then
17	// released before any socket I/O, so emission neither holds the lock across a
18	// syscall nor blocks the lock-free receive fast path. A concurrent Close closes
19	// the sockets out from under the snapshot; the resulting write error is benign
20	// (teardown) and dropped. It is a no-op when the bind has no probers or is closed.
21	func (m *Multipath) emitProbes() {
22		m.mu.Lock()
23		if len(m.paths) == 0 || m.probers == nil {
24			m.mu.Unlock()
25			return
26		}
27		type target struct {
28			ps *peerPathState
29			pr *telemetry.Prober
30		}
31		targets := make([]target, 0, len(m.paths))
32		for _, ps := range m.paths {
33			if ps.prober == nil {
34				continue
35			}
36			targets = append(targets, target{ps: ps, pr: ps.prober})
37		}
38		m.mu.Unlock()
39	
40		for _, t := range targets {
41			if remote, ok := t.ps.getRemote(); ok {
42				if raw, err := t.pr.SendProbe(); err == nil {
43					// UDP writes are goroutine-safe; this races no in-flight Send.
44					_, _ = t.ps.conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort(raw, remote)
45				}
46			}
47			t.pr.Tick()
48		}
49		// Eager failover nudge (defect D18): recompute the scheduler's active egress path
50		// each probe cadence so a liveness DOWN switches egress even when no application
51		// Send is driving Pick. This is the timer-driven companion to the receive-tick
52		// nudge (see tickLivenessFromReceive) — under CPU starvation this loop's ticker
53		// lags, which is why the receive-tick path carries the guarantee, but when the
54		// loop does run it keeps the selection fresh independent of egress traffic.
55		m.nudgeSchedulerActive()
56	}
57	
58	// StartProbeLoop launches the probe cadence goroutine: it calls emitProbes every
59	// interval until the returned stop function is invoked. The caller (device.Up)
60	// starts it AFTER the engine has opened the bind and stops it BEFORE Close, so the
61	// loop only runs while the sockets exist (emitProbes is a safe no-op if it races a
62	// closed bind). The returned stopper is idempotent.
63	//
64	// The cadence uses a wall-clock ticker because it is production timing glue, not
65	// liveness logic: every liveness decision the loop drives runs through the
66	// injected telemetry.Clock the Probers hold (SendProbe stamps it, Tick reads it),
67	// so tests drive emitProbes directly against a fake clock and never start this
68	// goroutine. It is a no-op (returning a no-op stopper) when the bind has no
69	// probers or interval <= 0.
70	func (m *Multipath) StartProbeLoop(interval time.Duration) (stop func()) {
71		if m.probers == nil || interval <= 0 {
72			return func() {}
73		}
74		// Arm the receive-path liveness sweep at the same cadence (D15): a receiver may
75		// now advance liveness when the timer goroutine below is starved under load.
76		m.sweepIntervalNanos.Store(int64(interval))
77		done := make(chan struct{})
78		go func() {
79			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
80			defer ticker.Stop()
81			for {
82				select {
83				case <-done:
84					return
85				case <-ticker.C:
86					m.emitProbes()
87				}
88			}
89		}()
90		var once sync.Once
91		return func() { once.Do(func() { close(done) }) }
92	}

861	// frame. The read buffer is private to this goroutine, so the per-path Codec's
862	// scratch is never shared.
863	//
864	// Owning the readers in the Bind (rather than returning one ReceiveFunc per path to
865	// the engine, as T12 did) is what makes a runtime path add/remove possible without
866	// disturbing the engine: the engine builds its receive goroutines ONCE from Open's
867	// return, so a path added later gets a reader HERE while the engine's fixed receive
868	// set — and thus the WG session — sees no churn. The goroutine exits when its socket
869	// is closed (RemovePath drains one path; Close drains all).
870	func (m *Multipath) readLoop(ps *peerPathState, deliver chan<- struct{}) {
871		defer m.readersWG.Done()
872		readBuf := make([]byte, maxDatagram)
873		for {
874			n, srcAP, err := ps.conn.ReadFromUDPAddrPort(readBuf)
875			if err != nil {
876				return // socket closed: this path was removed, or the bind was closed
877			}
878			// Per-path received-wire accounting (T23): count the OUTER datagram this path
879			// pulled off its socket before dispatch, lock-free. This goroutine is the sole
880			// writer of ps.rxBytes, so the atomic Add is uncontended.
881			ps.rxBytes.Add(uint64(n))
882			m.demuxInbound(ps, readBuf[:n], srcAP)
883			// Advance liveness off the receive path (throttled): a live signal on THIS path
884			// is what lets a DIFFERENT, silent path be marked DOWN promptly even when the
885			// probe-loop ticker is starved under load (D15). The peer probes every path
886			// (and floods the survivor after it roams), so the surviving path's reader
887			// supplies exactly that signal.
888			m.tickLivenessFromReceive(time.Now())
889			// Non-blocking poke: a single buffered slot coalesces bursts; the drainer
890			// re-checks the resequencer on every wake, so a coalesced poke loses nothing.
891			select {
892			case deliver <- struct{}{}:
893			default:
894			}
895		}
896	}

898	// tickLivenessFromReceive re-evaluates EVERY path's liveness from a receive
899	// goroutine, throttled to at most once per probe interval (T39, defect D15). It is
900	// the starvation-robust companion to StartProbeLoop's timer-driven Tick: the timer
901	// goroutine can be delayed ~1s under CPU saturation, but the receive goroutines are
902	// scheduled by the very traffic that must trigger failover, so liveness advances
903	// regardless. Ticking is monotone-safe: Tick only marks an UP path DOWN once its
904	// silence STRICTLY exceeds DownAfter and never brings a path UP (that needs
905	// RecordEcho), so a more frequent Tick can only make a genuine DOWN transition land
906	// sooner — never a premature/false one — and the failback hysteresis (owned by the
907	// scheduler) is untouched. No-op when the probe transport is absent (interval unset).
908	func (m *Multipath) tickLivenessFromReceive(now time.Time) {
909		interval := m.sweepIntervalNanos.Load()
910		if interval == 0 {
911			return // no probe transport / loop not started: nothing to Tick
912		}
913		n := now.UnixNano()
914		last := m.lastSweepNanos.Load()
915		if n-last < interval {
916			return // throttled: this interval's sweep was already taken
917		}
918		if !m.lastSweepNanos.CompareAndSwap(last, n) {
919			return // another receive goroutine won this interval's sweep
920		}
921		// Snapshot the prober set under m.mu (a runtime AddPath/RemovePath mutates it),
922		// then Tick OUTSIDE the lock so a transition's log write never runs under m.mu.
923		// TryLock, NOT Lock: Close holds m.mu WHILE it waits on readersWG for the readers to
924		// exit, so a reader that BLOCKED here on m.mu would deadlock that shutdown. The sweep
925		// is opportunistic and throttled, so when the lock is contended (a concurrent
926		// Close/AddPath/RemovePath/Send) simply skipping this interval is harmless — the
927		// probe-loop ticker and the next receive still advance liveness. This preserves
928		// Close's invariant that a reader never blocks on m.mu. The lock, when taken, is held
929		// at most once per interval (~5/s) for a bounded snapshot, so it adds negligible
930		// contention to Send and does not disturb the lock-free receive fast path.
931		if !m.mu.TryLock() {
932			return
933		}
934		probers := make([]*telemetry.Prober, 0, len(m.paths))
935		for _, ps := range m.paths {
936			if ps.prober != nil {
937				probers = append(probers, ps.prober)
938			}
939		}
940		m.mu.Unlock()
941		for _, pr := range probers {
942			pr.Tick()
943		}

946		// Bind calls ONLY from Send — so failover is pull-based: it happens only when the

950		// leaves both TCP directions stalled on the now-dead path, so NO Send occurs, so
951		// Pick is never called, so egress is never switched to the healthy backup — the
952		// bond wedges on the dead path until the 25s WG keepalive finally drives a Send.
953		// The receive tick is the starvation-robust signal that ALREADY detected the DOWN
954		// (it just Ticked the path down above); recomputing the scheduler from it makes the
955		// switch EAGER — bounded by the detection window (~DownAfter + one interval) rather

6		"errors"
7		"fmt"

9		"os/exec"

11	)
