13	// nsEnvMarker guards the one-shot re-exec below.
14	const nsEnvMarker = "WANBOND_E2E_NS"
15	
16	// TestMain re-execs the e2e test binary inside a FRESH network namespace so the
17	// fixtures never touch the host's networking. Under real root (the sudo target)
18	// it uses a plain mount+net namespace; unprivileged (the sandbox) it uses an
19	// unprivileged user+net namespace, which still grants CAP_NET_ADMIN for veth and
20	// netem. The PID-addressed peer namespace in netns.go needs no writable
21	// /run/netns, so this works in both environments.
22	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
23		if os.Getenv(nsEnvMarker) == "" {
24			self, err := os.Executable()
25			if err != nil {
26				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: cannot find test binary:", err)
27				os.Exit(1)
28			}
29			unshareArgs := []string{"-Urmn"}
30			if os.Geteuid() == 0 {
31				unshareArgs = []string{"-mn"}
32			}
33			args := append([]string{}, unshareArgs...)
34			args = append(args, "--", self)
35			args = append(args, os.Args[1:]...)
36	
37			cmd := exec.Command("unshare", args...)
38			cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), nsEnvMarker+"=1")
39			cmd.Stdin, cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = os.Stdin, os.Stdout, os.Stderr
40			err = cmd.Run()
41			if err == nil {
42				os.Exit(0)
43			}
44			var ee *exec.ExitError
45			if errors.As(err, &ee) {
46				os.Exit(ee.ExitCode())
47			}
48			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: namespace re-exec failed:", err)
49			os.Exit(1)
50		}
51		os.Exit(m.Run())
52	}

Match-all classifier in tc(8)        Linux        Match-all classifier in tc(8)

NAME
     matchall - traffic control filter that matches every packet

SYNOPSIS
     tc  filter  ...  matchall  [ skip_sw | skip_hw  ] [ action ACTION_SPEC ] [
             classid CLASSID ]

DESCRIPTION
     The matchall filter allows one to classify every packet that flows on  the
     port and run a action on it.

OPTIONS
     action ACTION_SPEC
            Apply  an  action  from  the  generic actions framework on matching
            packets.

     classid CLASSID
            Push matching packets into the class identified by CLASSID.

     skip_sw
            Do not process filter by software. If hardware has no offload  sup‐
            port  for  this filter, or TC offload is not enabled for the inter‐
            face, operation will fail.

     skip_hw
            Do not process filter by hardware.

EXAMPLES
     To create ingress mirroring from port eth1 to port eth2:

            tc qdisc  add dev eth1 handle ffff: clsact
            tc filter add dev eth1 ingress                \
                    matchall skip_sw                      \
                    action mirred egress mirror           \
                    dev eth2

     The first command creates a clsact qdisc with handle ffff: on device  eth1
     where  the  second  command attaches a matchall filters on it that mirrors
     the packets to device eth2 for ingress.

     To create egress mirroring from port eth1 to port eth2:

            tc qdisc add dev eth1 handle ffff: clsact
            tc filter add dev eth1 egress                  \
                    matchall skip_sw                       \
                    action mirred egress mirror            \
                    dev eth2

     The first command creates a clsact qdisc with handle ffff: on device  eth1
     where  the  second  command attaches a matchall filters on it that mirrors
     the packets to device eth2 for egress.

     To sample one of every 100 packets flowing into interface eth0 to  psample
     group 12:

            tc qdisc add dev eth0 handle ffff: ingress
            tc filter add dev eth0 parent ffff: matchall \
                 action sample rate 100 group 12

+// BlockEgress drops ALL outbound traffic on the named path's edge veth via a tc
+// clsact egress filter (matchall + drop), while leaving the veth up and inbound
+// traffic untouched — a one-way egress failure (e.g. a dead uplink amplifier), as
+// opposed to Blackhole's bidirectional link-down. It is used by T104 to prove
+// liveness is genuinely bidirectional: with only egress dead the peer's own probes
+// still ARRIVE at this path (an operator naively trusting "receiving traffic" as
+// health would stay wrongly up), but this path's OWN probe/echo round trip cannot
+// complete, so it must go DOWN. clsact attaches its own ingress/egress hooks
+// independent of whatever root qdisc is set, so it coexists with the path's netem
+// root qdisc (delay/jitter/loss) without disturbing it.
+func (top *Topology) BlockEgress(name string) {
+  p := top.path(name)
+  top.run("tc", "qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
+  top.run("tc", "filter", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "egress", "protocol", "all", "matchall", "action", "drop")
+// UnblockEgress removes a BlockEgress filter, restoring the named path's egress.
+// Idempotent — deleting an already-absent clsact qdisc is tolerated — so a test may
+// call it both explicitly and via t.Cleanup without failing on the second call.
+func (top *Topology) UnblockEgress(name string) {
+  p := top.path(name)
+  _ = top.tryRun("tc", "qdisc", "del", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
// Readdress replaces the edge-side address of the named path, simulating an edge
// public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4

1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strconv"
9		"strings"
10		"testing"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/metrics"
14	)
15	
16	// T104 (Q39, goals:G6) is an IN-GOAL VERIFICATION task, not a refactor: it asks
17	// whether an idle-but-"up" standby path's liveness is genuinely BIDIRECTIONAL, or
18	// whether it can be satisfied by receive-only traffic. The motivating production
19	// observation was wanbond_path_up{path="5g"}=1 with wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total{
20	// path="5g"}=0 — a standby path reported healthy while its tx counter never moved.
21	// Two independent checks:
22	//
23	//   - standby-transmits-when-idle: an UP standby that carries no DATA (active-backup
24	//     collapses all DATA onto the primary) must still be observed TRANSMITTING —
25	//     its own periodic liveness probes are real wire writes (bind.emitProbes ->
26	//     conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort) — while the primary carries a live flow.
27	//   - standby-egress-blocked-goes-down: with the standby's EGRESS direction (only)
28	//     blocked one-way at the edge veth (BlockEgress, a tc clsact/matchall/drop
29	//     filter — see netns.go), the standby must transition DOWN and must NOT be
30	//     selected when the primary is then killed. This is the affirmative half: it
31	//     proves the liveness verdict requires THIS path's own send-probe/receive-echo
32	//     round trip, not merely "traffic arrived on this interface" (the peer's own
33	//     probes keep ARRIVING at the blocked path the whole time — see
34	//     internal/bind/multipath.go dispatchInbound: an unechoed inbound PROBE only
35	//     learns the remote and is reflected; it never marks OUR liveness up — only
36	//     HandleEcho, driven by OUR OWN probe's authenticated echo, can).
37	//
38	// This harness cannot execute the `-tags e2e` netns tier itself (it requires
39	// CAP_NET_ADMIN/root and runs via the dedicated privileged target — see AGENTS.md);
40	// it is written to COMPILE and to make BOTH outcomes (pass, or a documented failure
41	// kept as a defect repro per Q39) observable once run there. A static read of the
42	// send path (internal/bind/probe.go's emitProbes never calls ps.txBytes.Add — only
43	// Send() and fecFlushDeadline() in internal/bind/multipath.go do) predicts the first
44	// subtest fails against the current implementation, reproducing the production
45	// observation; that is an inference from source, not a measured result — the
46	// authoritative answer is the hardware run's PASS/FAIL, at which point a genuine
47	// failure should be refiled as a defect linked to goals:G6 with this test kept as
48	// the repro, per the Q39 either-outcome acceptance.
49	const (
50		t104MetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9100"
51		t104MetricsURL    = "http://" + t104MetricsListen + "/metrics"
52	
53		// t104ProbeWindow is the idle-standby observation window for the tx-growth
54		// check: long enough (15 probe intervals at the default 200ms cadence) that
55		// even ONE counted probe would show up as a nonzero delta, well clear of
56		// scrape/scheduling jitter.
57		t104ProbeWindow = 15 * PLivenessProbeInterval
58	)
59	
60	// TestStandbyLivenessBidirectional runs both T104 checks. Each brings up its own
61	// topology (sequentially — the fixture's fixed veth names forbid two live
62	// topologies), mirroring the subtest structure of TestP2Aggregation.
63	func TestStandbyLivenessBidirectional(t *testing.T) {
64		bin := buildWanbond(t)
65	
66		t.Run("standby-transmits-when-idle", func(t *testing.T) {
67			testStandbyIdleTransmits(t, bin)
68		})
69		t.Run("standby-egress-blocked-goes-down", func(t *testing.T) {
70			testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown(t, bin)
71		})
72	}
73	
74	// testStandbyIdleTransmits is the FIRST T104 check: with the primary (starlink)
75	// carrying a live flow and the standby (cellular) carrying no DATA (active-backup
76	// default), the standby's wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total must still grow over a
77	// multi-probe-interval window — its own periodic liveness probes are genuine wire
78	// writes even though no DATA rides them.
79	func testStandbyIdleTransmits(t *testing.T, bin string) {
80		t.Helper()
81		top := Setup(t)
82		edge, conc := setupT104Tunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, "error")
83	
84		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
85			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
86		}
87	
88		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
89		standby := DefaultPaths[backupPathIdx]  // cellular
90	
91		waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 1, 10*time.Second)
92	
93		// Drive a live flow on the primary for (at least) the whole observation window;
94		// active-backup keeps ALL of it off the standby, so the standby's own tx (if
95		// any is counted) can only come from its periodic probes.
96		loadSecs := int(t104ProbeWindow.Seconds()) + 10
97		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
98		time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
99		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(loadSecs))
100		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond) // let the flow ramp before sampling the window
101	
102		before := scrapeMetrics(t, t104MetricsURL)
103		txBefore, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
104		if !ok {
105			t.Fatalf("edge /metrics missing %s{path=%q}", metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
106		}
107		if up, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, standby.name); !ok || up != 1 {
108			t.Fatalf("standby %q not up at window start (up=%v ok=%v)\n%s", standby.name, up, ok, edge.log())
109		}
110		if up, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, primary.name); !ok || up != 1 {
111			t.Fatalf("primary %q not up at window start (up=%v ok=%v)\n%s", primary.name, up, ok, edge.log())
112		}
113	
114		time.Sleep(t104ProbeWindow)
115	
116		after := scrapeMetrics(t, t104MetricsURL)
117		txAfter, ok := after.PathValue(metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
118		if !ok {
119			t.Fatalf("edge /metrics missing %s{path=%q} on the second scrape", metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
120		}
121		if up, ok := after.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, standby.name); !ok || up != 1 {
122			t.Fatalf("standby %q dropped from up during the idle window (up=%v ok=%v) — cannot judge idle-transmit liveness while down\n%s",
123				standby.name, up, ok, edge.log())
124		}
125	
126		delta := txAfter - txBefore
127		t.Logf("standby %q: wanbond_path_up=1 throughout a %s window with the primary carrying a live flow, tx delta = %.0f bytes",
128			standby.name, t104ProbeWindow, delta)
129		if delta <= 0 {
130			t.Errorf("standby path %q stayed wanbond_path_up=1 for the whole %s window while the primary carried a live "+
131				"flow, but its %s did not grow (delta=%.0f bytes) — this reproduces the production observation "+
132				"path_up{%s}=1 with tx{%s}=0. The standby's own periodic liveness probes ARE written to the wire "+
133				"(internal/bind/probe.go emitProbes -> conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort), but that write never increments "+
134				"ps.txBytes — only Send()/fecFlushDeadline() DATA/PARITY writes do (internal/bind/multipath.go). The "+
135				"path genuinely transmits; the operator-facing tx_bytes series just does not reflect it. Refile as a "+
136				"defect linked to goals:G6 (Q39) and keep this test as the reproduction.",
137				standby.name, t104ProbeWindow, metrics.MetricTxBytes, delta, standby.name, standby.name)
138		}
139	}
140	
141	// testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown is the SECOND T104 check: with the standby's
142	// egress (only) blocked one-way, it must transition DOWN — proving liveness needs
143	// this path's own probe/echo round trip, not merely inbound traffic arriving on it
144	// — and, once the primary is then killed, it must NOT be selected for failover.
145	func testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown(t *testing.T, bin string) {
146		t.Helper()
147		top := Setup(t)
148		// "info" so the scheduler's "active path change" transitions are readable from
149		// the edge log, the same idiom TestP1Failover uses.
150		edge, conc := setupT104Tunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, "info")
151	
152		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
153			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
154		}
155	
156		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
157		standby := DefaultPaths[backupPathIdx]  // cellular
158	
159		waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 1, 10*time.Second)
160	
161		// Block ONLY the standby's egress at the edge: outbound frames on its veth are
162		// dropped while the veth stays up and inbound (the concentrator's own probes)
163		// keeps arriving. A receive-only liveness check would stay wrongly up here.
164		top.BlockEgress(standby.name)
165		t.Cleanup(func() { top.UnblockEgress(standby.name) })
166	
167		waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 0, PLivenessDetectBudget)
168		t.Logf("standby %q went DOWN within %s of its egress being blocked one-way (its own probe/echo round trip broke; inbound traffic alone did not keep it up)",
169			standby.name, PLivenessDetectBudget)
170	
171		// Force a failover decision: kill the still-healthy primary. If the standby's
172		// DOWN verdict is genuine, active-backup has no healthy path left, so egress
173		// must NOT reroute onto the standby and connectivity must NOT recover.
174		top.Blackhole(primary.name)
175		t.Cleanup(func() { top.Restore(primary.name) })
176	
177		noFailoverWindow := PLivenessFailoverBudget + 3*time.Second
178		if top.pingUntil(concInner, noFailoverWindow) {
179			t.Errorf("connectivity recovered within %s after the primary died with the standby's egress still blocked "+
180				"— the falsely-live standby was selected for failover\n--- edge ---\n%s", noFailoverWindow, edge.log())
181		} else {
182			t.Logf("connectivity correctly did NOT recover within %s — the down standby was not selected", noFailoverWindow)
183		}
184		if idx := currentActivePathIdx(edge.log()); idx == backupPathIdx {
185			t.Errorf("edge scheduler switched its active path to the standby (index %d, %q) despite its egress being "+
186				"blocked and its liveness DOWN\n%s", backupPathIdx, standby.name, edge.log())
187		}
188	
189		// Sanity: the fixture recovers once both faults are cleared — a genuine
190		// liveness-driven exclusion, not a wedged/broken harness.
191		top.UnblockEgress(standby.name)
192		top.Restore(primary.name)
193		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
194			t.Fatalf("bond did not recover after clearing the egress block and restoring the primary\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
195				edge.log(), conc.log())
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// setupT104Tunnel brings the multipath tunnel up over paths with the (default,
200	// omitted) active-backup scheduler and the /metrics endpoint enabled on the edge —
201	// only the edge's tx/up series are asserted by either T104 check, so only the edge
202	// carries the [metrics] block. It otherwise mirrors setupMultipathTunnelLevel.
203	func setupT104Tunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, level string) (edge, conc *proc) {
204		t.Helper()
205	
206		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
207		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
208		psk := randKey(t)
209	
210		var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
211		for _, p := range paths {
212			fmt.Fprintf(&edgePaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
213			fmt.Fprintf(&concPaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
214		}
215		primary := paths[0]
216		metricsBlock := fmt.Sprintf("[metrics]\nlisten = %q\n\n", t104MetricsListen)
217	
218		dir := t.TempDir()
219		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
220	psk = "%s"
221	
222	%s%s[wireguard]
223	private_key = "%s"
224	
225	[[wireguard.peers]]
226	public_key = "%s"
227	endpoint = "%s:%d"
228	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
229	
230	[log]
231	level = %q
232	`, psk, edgePaths.String(), metricsBlock, edgePriv, concPub, primary.concIP, listenPort, concInner, level))
233	
234		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
235	psk = "%s"
236	
237	%s[wireguard]
238	private_key = "%s"
239	listen_port = %d
240	
241	[[wireguard.peers]]
242	public_key = "%s"
243	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
244	
245	[log]
246	level = %q
247	`, psk, concPaths.String(), concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner, level))
248	
249		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
250		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
251	
252		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
253			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
254		}
255		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
256			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
257		}
258		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
259		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
260		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
261		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
262		return edge, conc
263	}

- **Achieved:** PASS by the worker-visible gate: `gofmt -l cmd internal test` clean, `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e`, `go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e`, and `go test ./...` all green (all run via `nix develop -c sh`).
